3D Printed Lighting · Est. 2021

Light,
sculpted
by design.

Handcrafted lamps born from precision printing. Each piece is a unique intersection of geometry, material, and warm light — designed for spaces that refuse ordinary.

Our process

Our philosophy

Where form meets filtered light

Elden Designs was born from the belief that everyday objects deserve to be extraordinary. We combine the precision of 3D printing with Scandinavian design principles — restraint, warmth, and an obsession with material honesty.

Every lamp we produce starts as a sketch, becomes a model, and is printed layer by layer into an object that holds light the way a hand cups flame.

Custom pieces made

"We don't print lamps. We print atmospheres."

— Elden Design Studio

quiet spaces

How we work

From concept to
candlelit corner

01
Design

Every form is sketched by hand, then modelled with precision in 3D software — shaped by both intuition and geometry.

02
Print

Layer by layer, our FDM printers build each piece in premium PLA+ or PETG — materials chosen for warmth and longevity.

03
Finish

Each piece is hand-sanded, primed, and finished. Some receive a linen texture. Others stay raw — intentionally imperfect.

04
Deliver

Packed in recycled paper and delivered with a card detailing the story of your piece — its file name, print time, finishing date.

"The Lyra lamp is the first object in my home I've described as a presence. It doesn't just illuminate the room — it becomes the room."
— Ingrid M., Stockholm

Bespoke service

Your imagination,
our printer.

Have a space that needs something no catalogue can offer? We take custom commissions — from a single statement floor lamp to a full suite of matching pieces for an interior project.

Share a reference, a mood, a material. We'll handle the rest.

Elden Designs

3D printed lamps for interiors that value the space between objects as much as the objects themselves.
